_meta.comment: v2 expands from 3 to 8 seeded neighborhoods; full 35 still deferred to v1.5. Each entry gains a `faqs` array, a `nearby` array (for internal-link suggestions), and a `hero_photo` slug pointing at /images/stock/. Mirror of apps/quote/content/neighborhoods.json — keep in sync until packages/content/ extraction in v1.1.

neighborhoods.intro: Mt Pleasant is one of Vancouver's most-moved-into neighborhoods. Inlet Move Co. handles studio and one-bedroom moves between Mt Pleasant and the rest of Metro Vancouver every week. Same-week availability, hourly billing from $150/hr, $300 minimum. Quote in 60 seconds.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Most Mt Pleasant moves we run are studio and one-bedroom apartments going to Yaletown, Kitsilano, or East Van. Walk-up buildings are common; we handle the stairs without a stair fee.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: Apartment buildings along Main Street, Kingsway, and Quebec Street.

faqs.q: How much does a Mt Pleasant move usually cost?
faqs.a: A typical studio or one-bedroom Mt Pleasant move runs ~3.5 hours, billed at $150/hr with a $300 minimum. That puts most moves between $450 and $700 all-in, with no fuel surcharge, no stair fee, and no weekend upcharge.

faqs.q: Do you charge extra for walk-up buildings on Main Street?
faqs.a: No. Mt Pleasant has plenty of three- and four-storey walk-ups, and stairs are part of the job. We don't add a stair fee to the bill.

faqs.q: Can you move me from Mt Pleasant to Burnaby or the North Shore?
faqs.a: Yes. Inlet Move Co. covers all of Metro Vancouver — including Burnaby, Richmond, Surrey, North Vancouver, West Vancouver, Coquitlam, and New Westminster — at the same hourly rate.

neighborhoods.intro: Yaletown moves are typically condo-to-condo with elevator coordination. Inlet Move Co. handles building bookings and elevator pads as part of the standard hourly rate — no concierge fee, no elevator surcharge. Quote in 60 seconds; same-week availability.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Condo-to-condo within the downtown core, or downtown-to-suburb (North Van, Burnaby) for couples upgrading to larger spaces.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: Mainland Street, Pacific Boulevard, Davie Street.

faqs.q: Do you handle elevator-pad bookings for Yaletown buildings?
faqs.a: Yes. We can either coordinate with your concierge directly, or you book the freight elevator window and we arrive on it. There's no extra elevator fee.

faqs.q: What's the minimum-charge for a small Yaletown condo move?
faqs.a: Our minimum is $300 (two hours of two-mover crew). Most studio Yaletown moves still come in under that ceiling, so you're paying for time used.

faqs.q: Are weekend Yaletown moves more expensive?
faqs.a: No. We charge the same $150/hr seven days a week. Same-week Saturday slots fill up first, so request early.

neighborhoods.intro: Metrotown moves are often 2- and 3-bedroom moves into and out of high-rise condos. Inlet Move Co. handles two-mover crews + cargo van for hourly billing from $150/hr, $300 minimum. Multigenerational moves are a specialty — we're trained for households with extended family and ceremonial items.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Condo-to-condo within Burnaby, or Metrotown-to-Surrey for families upgrading to detached homes.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: Kingsway, Willingdon Avenue, Beresford Street, Imperial Street.

faqs.q: Do you do multigenerational moves out of Metrotown high-rises?
faqs.a: Yes — multigen moves are a specialty. We've trained the crew on handling ceremonial and religious items, and on coordinating across families during a single move.

faqs.q: How long does a 2-bedroom Metrotown move typically take?
faqs.a: Around 5 hours for a 2-bedroom condo, including elevator wait, loading, transit to a typical Metro Vancouver destination, and unloading. Bigger 3-bedroom moves run 6-8 hours.

faqs.q: Can you move from Metrotown to Surrey or Coquitlam in one trip?
faqs.a: Yes. Our cargo van handles 2- and 3-bedroom loads. The hourly rate covers transit time, so longer moves still come in at $150/hr.

neighborhoods.intro: Kitsilano sits on Vancouver's West Side, with a mix of heritage walk-ups and modern townhouse strata buildings. Inlet Move Co. runs same-week studio and one-bedroom moves in and out of Kits every week — $150/hr, $300 minimum, no stair fee, no weekend upcharge. Quote in 60 seconds.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Kits-to-downtown for couples shifting to a high-rise, or Kits-to-North-Shore when families upgrade to a detached home. Heritage three-storey walk-ups along West Broadway and West 4th are common.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: West 4th Avenue, West Broadway, Yew Street, Cornwall Avenue, Burrard Street.

faqs.q: Are most Kitsilano buildings walk-ups, or do they have elevators?
faqs.a: Mix of both. Heritage three- and four-storey walk-ups dominate near West 4th and Yew, while strata buildings further west have elevators. We handle either at the same $150/hr rate, no stair fee.

faqs.q: Do you do moves from Kitsilano to UBC or Point Grey?
faqs.a: Yes — student moves to UBC and family moves to Point Grey are routine. Both fall well inside our same-day Metro Vancouver radius.

faqs.q: Can you handle bike racks, surfboards, or paddleboards from Kits apartments?
faqs.a: Yes. Bikes are blanket-wrapped and strapped in the van. Long items (surfboards, paddleboards, ladders) we secure separately along the side wall — no extra charge.

neighborhoods.intro: North Vancouver crosses the Lions Gate Bridge from the rest of the Metro region — and that bridge crossing matters during rush hour. Inlet Move Co. plans North Van moves around bridge timing so you're not paying for bridge gridlock. $150/hr, $300 minimum, same-week availability.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Detached-to-detached across Lonsdale, or downtown-to-North-Shore for families upgrading. We also handle a steady stream of UBC-grad-to-North-Van condo moves in May and August.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: Lonsdale Avenue, 13th Street, Marine Drive, Capilano Road.

faqs.q: Does the Lions Gate Bridge add cost to a North Van move?
faqs.a: Bridge time is part of the hourly rate at $150/hr — no separate bridge surcharge. We schedule mid-morning or mid-afternoon to avoid rush-hour gridlock when possible.

faqs.q: Can you do a North Van move on a same-week basis?
faqs.a: Most weeks, yes. North Shore slots fill faster on Fridays and Saturdays — request a quote early in the week if you need a weekend slot.

faqs.q: Do you handle moves between West Vancouver and North Vancouver?
faqs.a: Yes. North Shore-internal moves (between West Van, North Van, and Deep Cove) are short and usually under our 4-hour zone.

neighborhoods.intro: Richmond moves run the spread from compact Brighouse condos to detached family homes near Steveston. Inlet Move Co. handles both ends of that range with one cargo van and a two-mover crew. Multigenerational moves are common in Richmond — our crew is trained to coordinate across generations and to handle ceremonial items with care.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Condo-to-condo within Brighouse and Lansdowne; detached moves between Steveston and central Richmond. Richmond-to-Burnaby and Richmond-to-Vancouver moves are routine.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: No. 3 Road, Westminster Highway, Granville Avenue, Steveston Highway, Williams Road.

faqs.q: Do you do multigenerational moves in Richmond?
faqs.a: Yes — multigen moves are something our crew is trained for. We coordinate with multiple decision-makers in the household and handle ceremonial and religious items with care.

faqs.q: Can you communicate in Mandarin or Cantonese during the move?
faqs.a: Mandarin and Cantonese support is on the roadmap. Today we run in English with respect for cultural-specific handling — full multi-language phone support ships once we partner with a verified translation reviewer.

faqs.q: How long does a Richmond-to-Vancouver move take?
faqs.a: About 4.5 hours for a typical one- or two-bedroom move, including loading, the No. 2 Road or Knight Street Bridge crossing, and unloading. Bigger detached homes run longer.

neighborhoods.intro: Surrey-Newton moves are typically 2- and 3-bedroom detached or townhouse moves, often with multigenerational households. Inlet Move Co. handles Newton at the same $150/hr rate as anywhere else in Metro Vancouver, with no upcharge for distance from Vancouver proper. Punjabi-language phone support is on the roadmap.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Townhouse-to-detached upgrades within Surrey, or Newton-to-Burnaby moves when families relocate closer to work. We also handle senior downsizing moves out of Newton family homes into smaller Surrey or Langley condos.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: King George Boulevard, 72nd Avenue, Scott Road, 80th Avenue.

faqs.q: Do you charge extra for moves from Surrey to Vancouver?
faqs.a: No. We bill at $150/hr regardless of where the move is in Metro Vancouver. Transit time across the Pattullo or Alex Fraser is included.

faqs.q: Can you do a multigenerational Newton move?
faqs.a: Yes. Multigen moves are a specialty. We coordinate with multiple household decision-makers and handle religious and ceremonial items per the family's instructions.

faqs.q: How long does a 3-bedroom Surrey move usually take?
faqs.a: Around 6 to 8 hours including loading, transit, and unloading. We finish most Newton moves the same day, even when the destination is across the bridge.

neighborhoods.intro: Coquitlam Town Centre is dense with newer high-rise condos around the SkyTrain stations, and detached neighborhoods spread across the surrounding hills. Inlet Move Co. handles both — $150/hr, $300 minimum, no upcharge for the longer drive into the Tri-Cities. Same-week availability for most weeks.
neighborhoods.common_moves: Coquitlam-to-Vancouver downtown moves for young professionals, and Coquitlam-to-Coquitlam upsize moves as families grow. We also handle Coquitlam-to-Port Moody short-haul moves at the same hourly rate.
neighborhoods.common_pickup_addresses_hint: Lougheed Highway, Pinetree Way, Glen Drive, North Road.

faqs.q: Is there an extra charge for moves all the way out to Coquitlam?
faqs.a: No. The $150/hr rate is flat across Metro Vancouver. Transit time from a Vancouver pickup to a Coquitlam destination is part of the hourly bill, not a separate fee.

faqs.q: Can you handle high-rise moves around Lincoln Station?
faqs.a: Yes. The newer Coquitlam Centre high-rises near Lincoln SkyTrain typically have freight elevators that need an advance booking — we coordinate that with your strata or building manager.

faqs.q: Do you do moves between Coquitlam and Port Moody or Port Coquitlam?
faqs.a: Yes. Tri-Cities-internal moves are short and usually run 3-4 hours. Same-week availability is strong for these routes.
